entities.description: saradc is a technology that is designed with 1 8v power supply and uses its own power as a reference voltage for operation
sar is a technology that refers to the successive approximation register used in the saradc for analog to digital conversion
adc is a technology that refers to the analog to digital converter used in the saradc for converting analog signals to digital codes
1 8v is a voltage measurement used as the power supply and reference voltage for the saradc
12 bit is a measurement of resolution used in the saradc for converting analog signals to digital codes
1msps is a measurement of the maximum conversion rate of the saradc
20mhz is a measurement of the clock frequency used in the saradc for analog to digital conversion
ov is a measurement of the input range for the saradc  typically from 0v to 1 8v

relations.description: saradc uses sar technology as its core component for analog to digital conversion
saradc is a type of adc that uses the successive approximation register for conversion
saradc is powered by and uses 1 8v as its reference voltage
saradc produces 12 bit binary digital codes from analog inputs
saradc has a maximum conversion rate of 1msps
saradc operates with a 20mhz clock frequency for conversion
saradc has an input range from 0v to 1 8v
